summary:
IMPORTANCE OF CONTINUAL SPIRITUAL PRAYER: Brandon emphasizes that the baptism of the Holy Spirit and speaking in tongues are essential for navigating the end times and achieving a successful Christian life. He shares his personal practice of praying in tongues throughout his daily activities, such as driving and doing yard work, explaining that this constant spiritual communication acts as a protective mechanism against demonic forces and provides continuous divine guidance. Brandon highlights that speaking in the Spirit allows believers to bypass language barriers during prayer and access a deeper level of overflow and spiritual power.

HISTORICAL EXAMPLES AND SCIENTIFIC VALIDATION: Brandon references several prominent religious figures, including Brother Hagin, Dr. Cho from South Korea, and Joel Osteen from Lakewood Church, noting that their dedication to praying in tongues for hours daily was the secret to their success. The text incorporates teachings from Sid Roth, who cites a 2008 University of Pennsylvania study demonstrating that speaking in tongues does not originate from the brain's frontal lobe. Roth explains that this practice aligns with biblical scriptures indicating that the spirit resides in the belly, allowing believers to pray perfect prayers with perfect faith while confounding demonic entities.

METHODOLOGY FOR UNCEASING PRAYER: Brandon shares instructional content on how to develop the habit of unceasing prayer. The method involves a progressive approach starting with praying audibly, transitioning to moving the lips silently, and eventually moving only the tongue without opening the lips. This technique allows individuals to pray from their spirit continuously while engaging in normal intellectual tasks or even sleeping. The teaching stresses that believers must initiate the physical act of speaking by faith, comparing it to Peter stepping out of the boat, rather than waiting to hear a spiritual language first. Brandon encourages individuals to renew their minds through scripture and actively pursue the baptism of the Holy Ghost to overcome spiritual bondage and walk in victory.
rawData: [November 25, 2023]-[Every christian should pray in tongues]-[https://youtu.be/dsdZwJfM26Q]-[Hey, good morning, family. I'm excited to be with you all again today. I just wanted to come on and tell you all thank you for all that you've done for us. Thank you for all the likes, the thumbs up, and the comments in the comment section. You are spreading the gospel throughout the world, literally, and I just want to tell you all thank you. I wanted to come on today and talk to you about the baptism of the Holy Ghost again. I was talking to Sid Roth on the phone a couple of days ago, and he just posted that video on his website about speaking in tongues. We were just talking about it, and I told him how much it blessed me. He said, "Brandon, if you want to, you can post that on your channel to bless the people." So I'm going to post that video right after I get finished talking to you right now about the baptism of the Holy Spirit. A lot of people don't believe in the baptism of the Holy Spirit, but I'm going to tell you: if it wasn't for the baptism of the Holy Spirit, I don't know where I would be. I pray in tongues all the time. People ask me, "How much prayer do you do in a day?" and honestly, it's different every single day. I don't try to make it into a religious habit where everything is in an ordered structure, like, "This is how much time you have to pray; you have to pray 30 minutes in the morning, 30 minutes in the evening." I pray in the Spirit whenever the Lord leads me to. I'll be driving down the road, praying in the Spirit. I pray in my house when I'm cleaning. I pray in tongues when I'm driving the mower around, or mowing, or pulling weeds, or raking leaves for the fall. I pray in the Spirit all the time, and I really truly believe praying in the Spirit is the key to having a successful, powerful Christian life. If you're not praying in the Spirit, it's like pouring water on your fire. You have to be praying in the Spirit all the time. If you want to walk in the supernatural in these last days, it's going to be by praying in the Spirit. If you are not praying in the Spirit, I really truly don't know how you function with your spiritual life; I really don't. And that's how bold I'm going to be about it, because I'm telling you, with all of the demonic things happening in the world and all the things going on, praying in the Spirit helps you to deal with this. It's like getting a download from the Holy Ghost on a minute-by-minute basis. You're filled with and controlled by the Holy Ghost, and that's how we operate in these end times. I'm telling you, if you're not praying in the Spirit and you're not baptized, speaking in tongues and doing that, you're just cutting your spiritual life short. That's all I can say. I don't really believe you can walk into your fullest overflow without the baptism of the Holy Ghost. Like I said, there have been times whenever I don't know how to pray, and you're struggling to find the words to ask the Father for whatever you're needing, and you don't know how to do it in English. So I start praying in tongues. A lot of people say, "Well, you need an interpreter." No, there are different types of praying in the Spirit. You're going to listen to Sid Roth here in just a second, and it will bless you. There are other people that teach on the baptism of the Holy Ghost, like Brother Hagin. Dr. Cho—he was in South Korea, and Sid Roth even talks about him—he prayed in tongues five hours a day, I believe is what he said. Joel Osteen was another man that prayed in tongues all the time. Joel Osteen had Lakewood Church down in the Houston area, and he prayed in tongues all the time. There are ministers of the gospel—what I'm talking about is that the key to the success of their ministries was praying in the Spirit. So, I hope you understand that it is very vital in these last days to pray in tongues all the time. I'm going to encourage you all: I have videos about praying in tongues. Pray along with me; motivate yourself to pray for 30 minutes, an hour, whatever. I have them on here. You can motivate yourself to pray, and you can pray alongside me; it will help motivate you and encourage you to pray in the Spirit. You can listen to it on the way to work, or all the way home from work. Sometimes you might have a bad day at work and you may need to pray all the way home because you just need to pray for your boss. I'm just trying to help you all, to let you know that when you are in a time of need or struggle, God will bring forth the Holy Ghost and fire. Out of your belly shall flow rivers of living water, and I'm telling you it will quench all the fiery darts of the wicked. When you quote the Word and get it down inside of your heart—when you memorize Psalm 91 and Psalm 23 and you get the Word inside of you and start quoting that Word out of your mouth—your words have power. He says, "As a man thinketh in his heart, so is he." So you start putting that inside of you; you change the way you think, you change "stinking thinking," you put in the Word, you're speaking in the Spirit, and you're praying in tongues. I'm telling you, your life will change overnight when you start praying in the Spirit. You'll look back in a week and go, "Wow, I am a different person," because you are praying mysteries out of your spirit every single time you do this. You're reading the Word, you're getting in the Word, you're praying in the Spirit, and you're being filled with the Holy Ghost on a continual basis. That is the key to success; that's the key to hearing the Holy Ghost like I do. I pray in the Spirit, and I read the Bible, and I pray some more. I want you to go ahead now and listen to this sermon, and this is going to really bless you. We'll see you in just a second. I'm going to close this out. "Welcome, Holy Spirit"—my favorite prayer. "Jesus, help." I've been studying, as many of you have, the lives of generals of the faith, and I found there was one major secret they had to their power, one common habit: they all spoke many hours in supernatural languages—tongues. One of my favorites that I read about was a pastor by the name of Dr. Cho in Korea. He had upwards of a million people going to his church. I can't even comprehend a million people going into a building—of course, they went in shifts, but still. This is what he said that really touched me: every day, the first thing he would do is pray six to eight hours in tongues. They said, "Dr. Cho, how can you take that time out of your day?" He said, "Because I could not have a church this large if I didn't take that time to pray in tongues." Paul says in Galatians 5, "But I say walk..."—and this is in the Amplified Classic—"I say, walk and live habitually..." That's a key word: walk and live habitually in the Holy Spirit. And then in 1 Thessalonians, he commands, "Pray without ceasing." I don't know if you ever saw that command; we ignore it. It's a command: "Pray without ceasing." But how can we live habitually in the Spirit? How can we pray without ceasing when you're sleeping or working? I mean, how do you do this? He wouldn't say to do it if there wasn't a way. This is where the supernatural comes in. I'm going to share a revelation in a moment that very few Christians have ever even heard, but that many believers have understood throughout the centuries. First, I want you to explain: why speak in tongues? I mean, what is the big advantage of speaking in tongues? Number one: God commands it. In 1 Corinthians 14:5, Paul says, "I desire that all speak in tongues." Pretty conclusive. Why speak in tongues? You're praying 100% from the Spirit. That means it's 100% perfect prayers. When you're praying from the mind, let's face it: we're humans, it's a mixture. But when you're praying from the spirit, you are praying perfect prayers with perfect faith. Why do you have perfect faith? You don't know what you're praying; your brain isn't getting in the way. You say, 'Well, that's impossible.' Of course, God says nothing is impossible. Another reason: when you pray out loud, the devil and all the demons hear you and they try to obstruct what you're doing. But they go—there's a Hebrew word, meshuggah, a little crazy—when you pray in tongues. Because unless God reveals what you're praying, you don't even know what you're praying. They're called unknown tongues for a reason: unknown to the devil as well as people. When you pray in tongues, you're edifying your spirit man. The word edify in the Greek means you are charging your battery, you are lifting weights in the gym, and you are building muscle in your spirit man where you need it. It says in 1 Corinthians, when you pray in tongues, you praise God. Well, who wouldn't want to praise God? Well, I would. The most important reason Paul talks about—a lot of people miss it; this isn't the point I'm going for, but I want to explain this—do you know how much revelation Paul had? That's where he got his revelation. Paul spoke in tongues. 1 Corinthians 14:18, again, the Amplified Classic. I always felt this, but then I saw it in print. I never realized it; most of the Bibles I see say, 'I speak in tongues more than any of you,' but the original Greek says, 'more than all of you put together.' Now, he's talking to the Corinthian Church, which was very fanatical on the gifts of the Spirit, and he's saying, 'You think you guys are fanatical? I speak in tongues more than all of you put together.' It had to be important; that's where he got his revelation from. Recently, I discovered Paul's hidden secret about how to have habitual fellowship with God and how to pray without ceasing. First, there's scientific evidence. In 2008, the University of Pennsylvania released findings that prove the practice of speaking in tongues does not come from the brain. In the study, participants' brain activity was monitored while they spoke in tongues—and you can look this up on the internet—it's not coming from the part of the brain, the frontal lobe, where speech in the languages you know comes from. That's what you've been taught. Second, there's a biblical explanation. The Bible says, Paul says, 'I will pray with my spirit by the Holy Spirit that is within me, but I will also pray intelligently with my own mind and my own understanding.' That really distinguishes it. Everyone knows where the mind is, but not everyone, even believers, knows where your spirit is. Again, Jesus actually told us where our spirit is. Most people go here, to the heart. No, Jesus said, 'Out of your innermost belly will flow rivers of living water.' So I pray with my understanding—my mind—and I pray from my spirit—my belly. Yes, your spirit is in your belly. In 1 Thessalonians, Paul commands us to pray without ceasing. I believe you can and will pray without ceasing. I'll be right back. Has that ever happened to you? Praying in tongues and travail gets so deep you can't even utter the words. You literally birth things in your life in the spirit. It's going to happen more and more to people that pray in tongues. I have in my hand one of the most valuable books we've ever done. I really want to give you this absolutely free from my heart. One of the biggest regrets I have is that I wish I had known the great pioneers of our faith. All the great ones had a secret that most Christians don't know: they prayed in tongues without ceasing. I didn't realize how valuable that gift was. I spoke in tongues, but not as much as I should have. I am convinced that by speaking in supernatural languages, I edify my spirit. And you know what? That was the secret to their power, the secret to their wisdom, the secret to their proximity with God. I don't want you to have to be like me, to wait until you're my age to realize what a wonderful gift this is. I have put fifty years of my personal experience with supernatural languages in this book, everything that I've learned. The name of the book is PT: Personal Trainer Tongues. When you finish reading this book, even if you've never prayed in supernatural languages in your life, I promise you, if you follow the instructions—and I've read it and read it—some of these mysteries won't be mysteries in your life anymore. Father God, in Yeshua's name, we so welcome you, Holy Spirit, today. I pray that everyone participating realizes the magnitude and never takes this for granted—to take your words, your messages, and your gospel to the four corners of the earth. In Jesus' name, Amen. We now return to 'It's Supernatural.' I remember years ago as a young Jewish believer, I observed a great man of God, Kenneth Hagin Senior. I would go to his conferences and I would notice when he wasn't speaking and he was seated on the platform, he'd always have his hands together like this and his lips would be moving. Then I found out from someone I interviewed, Dr. Norvel Hayes, who is also now in heaven—he was on his board and a personal friend of Dr. Hagin. And he found out from Dr. Hagin that speaking in tongues without ceasing was a secret. I'll give you a quick story. His son shares this: one afternoon they're watching a good football game, and he looks over at his dad, and his dad's lips are moving. 'Dad, chill out! This is football, this is fun!' He looks at his son and tells him that was his secret. As Dr. Hagin's secret went to the grave with most of them, how do you pray without ceasing? This is what I've learned. First of all, you can pray in English out loud, and you can pray in English almost thinking it without even mouthing it, can't you? That's from your brain. How much more should you be able to pray from your spirit? And this is what I've been doing: I have been praying without making a sound, and then I found I could pray by moving my tongue. In tongues, I'm saying, without moving my lips. I heard a judge in Fairfax, Virginia, say with my own ears, 'When I do a case, I'm praying in tongues without ceasing because I need wisdom beyond myself.' So I started doing it without moving my tongue and without moving my lips. I started first speaking without making an audible sound, and I started moving my tongue and moving my lips. Then, eventually, step two was not moving my lips and just moving my tongue. And step three, it started becoming a habit. You know how you pray without ceasing in your sleep? If you pray hours and hours without ceasing while you're awake, guess what your spirit man does the whole time that you're sleeping? Now, the best way I know to make a habit of this is to, number one, make sure that Jesus is your Messiah and Lord. Number two, ask God to fill you completely. You see, you couldn't make Jesus your Messiah and Lord unless the Holy Spirit was in you. Contrary to a lot of teaching, let no one tell you the Holy Spirit's not in you if you don't speak in tongues. Come on now! You are only led to the Lord by the Holy Spirit coming upon you; it's the only way you can do it. So first, you pray to make Jesus your Messiah and Lord. Usually, the Bible actually says out loud, something about you hearing your own self speak out loud to your spirit man making Jesus your Lord. Then the second thing is you're filled with the Spirit and you have the gift to speak in tongues. Here's the greatest analogy I've ever seen with the gift to speak in tongues: it takes faith. Do you know it actually says speaking in tongues is the least of the gifts? Do you know why it says that? It's the easiest to manifest. That's why it says that: it's the easiest for you to enter the supernatural realm where all the gifts are. So there was a Jewish guy by the name of Peter, and Peter was in a boat and he saw Jesus walking on the water and he said, 'Hey, that's pretty cool, Jesus, I'd like to do that.' And Jesus surprised him: He said, 'Come.' And then Peter said, 'I will only come if you move my legs and give me the supernatural ability to walk on water.' No! You see, he could not walk on water and he knew it; only Jesus could help him walk on water, but he knew how to move his own legs. That's what people do with their prayer language: 'No, I'm not going to speak until I hear it.' Well, then God can't do His part. God always cooperates with man. There's a part for man to play and a part for God to play. And I have found everyone that doesn't manifest their prayer language, even though they have that prayer language inside of them, is because they're not willing to do what they've been doing their whole life: speak. The only difference is you're going to speak before you hear it. Why won't you hear it first? Because it's not here; it's not in the brain. You will speak and it'll come out of you. 'Oh, I'm not going to do that, I might make it up.' Well, just remember whose name you prayed in: Jesus. Every knee shall bow, every tongue confess; your tongue will confess. That's what it says. Now, when I pray with you—so step two, filled with the Holy Spirit. If you're filled with the Holy Spirit, you don't have to speak in tongues, but you have the ability to speak in tongues. You have the ability and you can do your part, but you'll never see God's part, Peter, if you don't move your own legs and get out of that boat. So those at home and those in the studio audience, and then we're going to go into the third phase: how to pray without ceasing. By doing what I said, you start praying silently, move your lips, move your tongue, and then you close your lips and don't move them. So you'll be like that judge that needed supernatural wisdom. You know, you can have a complete intellectual conversation with someone and be praying unceasingly. Why? One's coming from the Spirit, the other's from the brain. They even proved it scientifically; I just told you about the study. So let's start out by reaffirming, or for the first time—a lot of you have made Jesus your Savior, rightfully so—now it's time to make him your Savior and Lord. That's the only gear we've been trained to; it's Savior and Lord. In the Bible, it's not just Savior. Repeat after me out loud; mean it to the best of your ability at home and right here: 'Dear God, I've made many mistakes for which I'm so sorry. I believe the blood of Jesus washed them away and I'm clean. And now that I'm clean, Jesus, come and live inside of me. I make you my Savior. You saved me from my sins, and now I make you my Lord. Amen.' Let's conclude by praying out loud in tongues now. I'll give you a clue: when God is happy, I start laughing in the spirit. If you do it as quickly as your tongue can move, you'll break into a new language. You've been praying in tongues—one language—the last 30 years. Break into your new language as quickly as your tongue can move. Well, I hope that sermon blessed you; it sure blessed me. So now let's just pray together for a second. I want you all to bow your heads. Father, I just thank you, Lord, right now. I believe that they are filled with and controlled by the Holy Spirit concerning their lives. We bind the devil, we bind confusion, we bind the spirit of torment in the name of Jesus off of their minds. We command you in the name of Jesus, you spirits of doubt and unbelief and religion, we command you to cease and desist. We command you to release their thoughts right now in the name of Jesus. And we thank you, Lord, that they have the power of the Holy Ghost flowing through them right now with the evidence of speaking in other tongues. And I thank you, Lord, right now we take authority over religious spirits, demonic spirits that would try to come and hinder them from receiving this beautiful gift. We thank you, Lord, that you've delivered them from this and you are filling them up right now. We call them healed and whole and well by the stripes of Jesus. We call them to have a renewed mind by reading the Word and renewing their mind on a day-to-day basis, getting in the spirit, getting in the Holy Ghost by filling yourself up with the Word of God. And I thank you, Lord, that you said in your Word that we can have what we say according to your scripture, Father. I thank you, Lord, that faith calls those things that be not as though they were. And Father, we thank you, Lord, that they are filled with the Holy Ghost right now. We thank you, Lord, that they are delivered from satanic bondage, from strongholds, from things that have been trying to bind them up. Father, I thank you, Lord, that your Word says that for whom the Son has set free is free indeed, and we call them free right now. We call them delivered right now. We call them set free, we call their minds renewed. We know, Father, that nothing's too big for you. Nothing's too big for you, God. You are the God of more than enough, and we thank you, Lord, that you're the healer, you're the deliverer, and you will set us all free, Father, because you said it according to Galatians: 'Christ has redeemed you from the curse of the law and we're not under the curse anymore.' And I just want to say to you all, thank you for watching this video and I hope it blessed you today. I hope this gave you more of an inspiration to be baptized in the Holy Ghost because I'm telling you all, if you want to walk in victory in these last days, you've got to be filled with the Holy Ghost. Some people say, 'Well, I'm having a hard time with it.' You have to get out of your thoughts, you have to get out of your thinking and just flow in the Holy Ghost. It's going to sound weird to your natural ear, but you start praying—that's what I do—we just start praying in the Holy Ghost. I can turn it on and off, boom, boom, boom. We just go in there and we go, 'Father, we just thank you.' And as I was praying, the Lord said, 'Tell them to go down there and listen about being baptized in the Holy Ghost.' You've already—I've already done another video on this, but this is how important I feel that it is that you get this gift. So I'm going to keep talking about it, but I'm telling you, I've already done another video, I've done several videos on praying in the Holy Ghost. You go find them down below and they'll bless you about being baptized in the Holy Ghost. So that's all I really have for you all today. I'll have another video here in a little bit, but this is the one that was important to me because I kept feeling in my heart the Lord said, 'Brandon, I want you to bring this video out for them right now.' So put a smile on your face and a song in your heart. Jesus loves you so much and we will see you next time. You all have a blessed day. Bye-bye.]
